title: Migrate Klaviyo to GoHighLevel — Complete Agency Guide
description: Migrate from Klaviyo to GoHighLevel: Complete step-by-step guide for Indian agencies. Export contacts, setup WhatsApp automation, consolidate tools. Save ₹50...
html_content:
🚀 Try GoHighLevel FREE for 30 days — No credit card required. Start your free trial here →

Migrate Klaviyo to GoHighLevel — Complete Agency Guide for Indian Digital Marketers

You're managing three separate platforms right now: Klaviyo for email, WhatsApp Business API for customer messaging, and a separate CRM. Your team in Bangalore is frustrated with tool switching. Your Mumbai clients are asking why their SMS campaigns can't talk to their email lists. And you're paying ₹50,000+ monthly just to keep everything running.

This is the exact situation hundreds of Indian digital agencies face today.

The Real Cost of Platform Fragmentation

Klaviyo is powerful for email marketing — no argument there. But it was built for e-commerce teams in Western markets. It doesn't prioritize WhatsApp automation (the lifeline of Indian customer communication), it doesn't integrate seamlessly with Razorpay or PayU for payment tracking, and it forces you to maintain separate tools for CRM, landing pages, and SMS.

When you're running a lean agency in Delhi, Hyderabad, or Pune, tool fragmentation costs you more than money. It costs you:

  • Time — your team spends 10+ hours weekly switching between platforms and manually syncing data
  • Data loss — customer journeys break when tools don't talk to each other
  • Revenue leakage — you can't offer unified marketing automation to clients without building custom solutions
  • Scalability friction — onboarding new team members means training them on 4-5 different systems

What if you could consolidate everything into one platform? Email, WhatsApp, SMS, CRM, landing pages, funnels, and client reporting — all integrated, all tracked, all in one dashboard.

Enter GoHighLevel: Built for Agencies Like You

GoHighLevel isn't another email tool. It's an all-in-one platform built specifically for agencies and entrepreneurs who need to move faster and charge more. More importantly for Indian agencies, it's built with WhatsApp automation baked in from day one.

The platform lets you:

  • Automate customer journeys across WhatsApp, email, and SMS from a single interface
  • Build landing pages and sales funnels without developers
  • Track payments from Razorpay/PayU directly in your CRM
  • Manage unlimited client sub-accounts (perfect for agencies managing 20+ clients)
  • Generate GST-compliant invoices and reports for Indian tax compliance
  • Scale your team without licensing costs — flat rate for the whole agency

And the pricing? GoHighLevel Starter at $97/month (approximately ₹8,000/month) replaces what most agencies spend ₹50,000-75,000/month on across Klaviyo, separate CRM, SMS tools, and landing page builders.

Real Example: A Bangalore E-Commerce Agency's Migration

Nitya runs a digital agency in Bangalore managing 15 D2C e-commerce brands. She was spending ₹60,000/month on Klaviyo + Zoho CRM + a separate WhatsApp messaging service. Her team couldn't see the full customer journey — email metrics lived in Klaviyo, WhatsApp conversations in her messaging app, and order data in Zoho.

After migrating to GoHighLevel's Agency plan ($297/month — approximately ₹24,700/month), she:

  • Consolidated all customer data into one CRM view
  • Built WhatsApp automations for order confirmations, shipping updates, and abandoned cart recovery
  • Created reusable funnels her team now sells as services to clients
  • Reduced platform costs by 60% while improving campaign performance
  • Freed up 8 hours weekly her team used to spend on manual data syncing

Her clients now see unified marketing reporting. Her team onboards in days, not weeks. And she's added ₹2,00,000/month in new revenue by offering GoHighLevel-powered automation services to existing clients.

The Step-by-Step Migration Process

Step 1: Audit Your Klaviyo Account

Before you export anything, document what you have:

  • Total contacts and segments
  • Active automations and email flows
  • Custom properties and fields
  • List structure and naming conventions
  • Active campaigns and their performance metrics

This takes 2-3 hours but prevents migration headaches. Export a sample of contacts first to review data quality.

Step 2: Export Contacts and Segments from Klaviyo

In Klaviyo, go to Audience → Lists and download each segment as a CSV file. Include all custom fields your clients use (e.g., purchase history, product preferences, lifecycle stage).

Name your exports clearly: contacts_vip_segment_jan2025.csv rather than export1.csv. You'll thank yourself later.

Step 3: Import into GoHighLevel

In your GoHighLevel account, go to CRM → Contacts → Import. Upload your CSV files. GoHighLevel automatically maps common fields (email, phone, name) and lets you manually assign custom fields.

Pro tip for Indian agencies: If you're importing phone numbers, ensure they're formatted with the country code (+91 for India). GoHighLevel will recognize them for WhatsApp automation instantly.

Step 4: Recreate Segments and Lists

In GoHighLevel, segments work via Tags and Smart Lists. Instead of maintaining multiple static segments like Klaviyo, create dynamic Smart Lists that auto-update based on customer behavior. For example:

  • "High-value customers" (tag + purchase amount > ₹10,000)
  • "Dormant customers" (tag + no purchase in 90 days)
  • "WhatsApp engaged" (tag + opened WhatsApp message in last 7 days)

Step 5: Rebuild Automations with WhatsApp Focus

This is where GoHighLevel shines for Indian agencies. Instead of separate email and SMS flows, create unified journeys that use WhatsApp as the primary channel.

Example automation: Customer purchases → WhatsApp order confirmation → Email invoice → SMS tracking update → WhatsApp review request (3 days later).

Set up these core automations first:

  • Welcome series (WhatsApp + email)
  • Abandoned cart recovery (WhatsApp first, email backup)
  • Post-purchase follow-up (order updates via WhatsApp)
  • Win-back campaign (for inactive contacts)

Step 6: Test and Monitor

Before going live, send test messages to your own WhatsApp and email. Verify that:

  • WhatsApp messages are delivering with correct formatting
  • Unsubscribe links work
  • Payment integrations (Razorpay/PayU) are tracked
  • Reporting shows accurate metrics

GoHighLevel Pricing: The ROI Breakdown for Indian Agencies

Plan Monthly Cost Best For
Starter $97 (~₹8,000) Solo entrepreneurs, small agencies (1-5 clients)
Agency $297 (~₹24,700) Growing agencies (10-50 clients), unlimited sub-accounts

Compare this to Klaviyo ($20-300/month) + Zoho CRM (₹2,000-5,000/month) + WhatsApp Business messaging (₹1,500-3,000/month) = you're likely spending ₹50,000-75,000/month for fragmented tools.

GoHighLevel's Agency plan at ₹24,700/month consolidates everything and gives you white-label capabilities to resell to clients. If you charge clients just ₹5,000/month for managed WhatsApp automation, 5-10 clients pay for your entire platform.

WhatsApp Automation: India's Unfair Advantage

Here's what most Klaviyo migration guides miss: GoHighLevel was built with WhatsApp as a primary channel, not an afterthought.

In your Automations builder, you can:

  • Send templated WhatsApp messages triggered by customer actions
  • Receive WhatsApp replies and auto-respond based on keywords
  • Store WhatsApp conversations in contact profiles
  • Track WhatsApp delivery and read rates
  • Combine WhatsApp with email/SMS in multi-channel journeys

No APIs. No third-party tools. No integration nightmares.

FAQ: Common Concerns from Indian Agencies

Will I lose my Klaviyo campaign history?

GoHighLevel doesn't import Klaviyo's reporting history — only contacts and lists. But you can export your campaign data from Klaviyo before migration. Going forward, all metrics live in GoHighLevel. Your team will have unified reporting from day one.

What if I have complex custom fields in Klaviyo?

GoHighLevel supports custom fields and properties. During import, you'll map your Klaviyo fields to GoHighLevel. Simple fields (text, dropdown, date) migrate directly. Complex fields (conditional logic) may need slight rebuilding — usually 2-3 hours of work.

Can I run both platforms during migration?

Yes. Most agencies run parallel migrations for 30-60 days. Send new signups to GoHighLevel while keeping Klaviyo active for existing campaigns. Once you've validated everything works, sunset Klaviyo. This reduces risk significantly.

Does GoHighLevel handle GST compliance for Indian billing?

GoHighLevel integrates with Razorpay and PayU, both of which are GST-compliant in India. You can generate invoices with GST classification directly from your CRM for client billing.

How long does a full migration typically take?

For most agencies: 1-2 weeks. Step 1-3 (export/import) takes 2-3 days. Step 4-5 (rebuild automations) takes 5-7 days. Step 6 (testing) takes 3-5 days. Larger agencies with 100k+ contacts might take 3-4 weeks, but the work is mostly asynchronous.

The Bottom Line

Migrating from Klaviyo to GoHighLevel isn't about switching email platforms. It's about consolidating your entire marketing and CRM stack into a single, unified system built for agencies.

You'll spend 1-2 weeks on migration and save 60-70% on platform costs. Your team will spend 8+ hours weekly on actual client work instead of platform management. Your clients will see better results because their entire customer journey is now visible in one dashboard.

If you're managing more than 3 clients or spending more than ₹30,000/month on marketing tools, this migration will pay for itself in the first 30 days.

Ready to start? Launch your free 30-day GoHighLevel trial today. Test the platform with your own data, build one automation, and see the difference unified marketing automation makes for your agency.

Pro tip: The HighLevel LIVE India 2026 community includes hundreds of agencies running GoHighLevel. Join the conversation, share your migration experience, and learn from teams already scaling their agencies on this platform.
